title: Delivery Semantics & EOS
learningObjectives: Implement **idempotent** consumers using **business** keys and **database** constraints
Explain **transactional** read-process-write scopes and operational limits
Design **outbox** hybrids for **atomic** DB writes and **Kafka** publication
Diagnose **duplicate** incidents with reconciliation metrics and replay tests
Choose **dedupe** storage (cache vs database) based on criticality

sections.title: Why Delivery Semantics & EOS matters
sections.content:
Delivery semantics are where **brokers** meet **banks**. Interviewers want **at-least-once** + **idempotent** effects articulated clearly, not buzzword **EOS**.

You should connect **offsets**, **transactions**, and **outbox** patterns to **metrics** finance can **reconcile**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Delivery Semantics & EOS
sections.content:
### Day 62 — **Delivery** **semantics** and **EOS**

### 1. Semantics ladder
**At-most-once**, **at-least-once**, **effectively-once**, **Kafka** **transactional** guarantees.

### 2. Idempotency
**Business** keys, **unique** constraints, **state machine** transitions.

### 3. Transactions
**consume-process-produce** isolation costs and operational limits.

### 4. Outbox
Pairs **DB** durability with **event** publication.

### 5. Failure cases
**Partial** **commit**, **duplicate** **retries**, **replays**.

### 6. Story
**Retry** storm duplicates charges → **unique** **operation_id** saves the quarter.

sections.title: Basic — Semantics ladder
sections.output:
at-most-once: may lose, will not duplicate (idealized)
at-least-once: may duplicate, will not lose (with care)
effectively-once: at-least-once + idempotent side effects

sections.title: Intermediate — Dedupe key toy
sections.description: Build dedupe key from topic, partition, offset.

sections.title: Advanced — Idempotent flag map
sections.description: Simulate seen event ids with a set.

sections.title: Outbox + consumer idempotency
sections.description: DB transaction anchors event; consumer dedupes by business key.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 62 — EOS patterns
database DB
queue Kafka
participant Service
Service -> DB : txn + outbox row
Service -> Kafka : async publish drain
Kafka -> Service : consume with idempotent handler
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Delivery semantics — dedupe key (Day 62 assignment)
hints: Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.
State a **failure mode** (e.g. rebalance, ISR shrink, schema break) tied to your answer.

sections.title: Use cases — Delivery Semantics & EOS
sections.content:
## Use cases — **Delivery semantics & EOS**

### 1) **Inventory** **reservation** **service**
**Context:** **Reserve** stock on **event**; **cancel** on **timeout**; **no** **double** **reserve**.
**Why Day 62:** **At-least-once** + **retries** need **idempotent** **commands**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Dedupe** table keyed by **reservation** **request** **id**.
2. **Outbox** for **Kafka** + **DB** **atomicity** where needed.
3. Define **exactly-once** **scope** (partition? **service**? **end-to-end**?).
**Failure hook:** **Duplicate** **reservation** after **consumer** **retry**.

### 2) **Payment** **settlement** **worker**
**Context:** **Read** **Kafka** → **call** **PSP** → **emit** **result**.
**Why Day 62:** **Side** **effects** **outside** **Kafka** break naive **EOS** claims.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Idempotent** **PSP** **keys** + **reconciliation** **job**.
2. **Transactional** **read-process-write** only if **all** **parts** support it.
3. **DLQ** + **manual** **replay** playbook for **poison** **messages**.
**Failure hook:** **PSP** **timeout** → **duplicate** **capture** without **keys**.

### 3) **Stream** **join** **billing** **with** **usage**
**Context:** Join **subscription** **events** with **metering** **stream**.
**Why Day 62:** **Time** **alignment** and **reprocessing** semantics.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Watermarks** / **grace** for **late** **data** policy.
2. **Version** **state** **stores** for **replay** after **bug** **fix**.
**Failure hook:** **Duplicate** **join** **output** after **changelog** **replay**.

### 4) **GDPR** **delete** **propagation**
**Context:** **Right** **to** **erasure** must **reach** **derived** **stores**.
**Why Day 62:** **Tombstones** + **compaction** + **consumer** **handling**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Emit **tombstone** on **PII** **delete** **topic**.
2. **Consumers** **purge** **downstream** **indexes**.
3. Validate **retention** vs **legal** **hold**.
**Failure hook:** **Compacted** **topic** **replay** **resurrects** **deleted** **rows** if **consumers** **skip** **nulls**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: Define **at-most-once** in Kafka-shaped systems.
conceptual.answer: Offsets **committed** **before** processing finishes or messages skipped → **loss** **possible**. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Define **at-most-once** in Kafka-shaped systems.**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Define **at-least-once**.
conceptual.answer: Processing may run **multiple** **times** for same message; **dedupe** or **idempotency** needed downstream. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Define **at-least-once**.**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Define **exactly-once** in **practice**.
conceptual.answer: **End-to-end** EOS requires **Kafka** **protocol** features **plus** **external** **side-effect** **idempotency**. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Define **exactly-once** in **practice**.**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What does **idempotent** consumer mean?
conceptual.answer: Second **delivery** of same **business** **event** does **not** change **final** **state** thanks to **keys** or **constraints**. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What does **idempotent** consumer mean?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Transactional** **read-process-write** sketch?
conceptual.answer: Consume, transform, produce, commit **offsets** and **messages** **atomically** in **Kafka** **transaction** **scope**. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****Transactional** **read-process-write** sketch?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why **business** **eventId** beats **offset-only** dedupe?
conceptual.answer: Offsets change on **replay** or **topic** **migration**; **business** **id** survives **reconciliation**. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Outbox** pattern role?
conceptual.answer: **Atomically** **persist** **event** with **DB** **txn** then **async** **publisher** drains **outbox** safely. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Duplicate** window from **producer** **retries**?
conceptual.answer: Without **idempotence**, same **logical** event can **appear** multiple times. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****Duplicate** window from **producer** **retries**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Two-phase** commit between DB and Kafka?
conceptual.answer: Rare; heavy; **outbox** or **change-data-capture** often simpler operationally. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

conceptual.question: **Clock skew** dedupe risk?
conceptual.answer: Time-window **dedupe** alone **misses** **slow** duplicates or **reordered** clocks. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

conceptual.question: **Saga** vs **Kafka** EOS?
conceptual.answer: **Saga** coordinates **multiple** **services**; **Kafka** tx covers **consumer+producer** to **broker**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Poison pill** handling?
conceptual.answer: Skip with **DLQ** after **N** tries while **auditing** **offset** advance strategy. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

conceptual.question: What is **effectively-once** phrasing?
conceptual.answer: **At-least-once** transport + **idempotent** **effects** = **effective** EOS for the business. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

conceptual.answer: **Classic** **pattern** for **at-least-once** with **no** **loss** when DB fails before commit. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

conceptual.question: **Debezium** + **Kafka** duplication?
conceptual.answer: **CDC** may **replay**; consumers must **handle** **duplicate** **logical** rows. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

conceptual.question: When is **static consumer assignment** preferable to **subscribe**?
conceptual.answer: When you need **deterministic** partition ownership, **pin** workloads, or avoid **rebalance** disruption for specialized consumers—at the cost of **manual** scaling and **no** automatic balance. Misuse causes **double consumption** or **missed** partitions.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **When is **static consumer assignment** preferable to **subscribe**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: How do **controller** elections impact clients during broker failures?
conceptual.answer: Clients rely on **metadata** for leaders. Controller work schedules **leader** changes; clients **refresh** metadata and may see **NOT_LEADER** spikes. **Watch** metadata request rates and **client** retry storms during incidents.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **How do **controller** elections impact clients during broker failures?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

codeBased.question: Idempotent insert pseudo-SQL.
codeBased.answer: // INSERT .. ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING with business event_id unique

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// INSERT .. ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING with business event_id unique** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?

followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// producer send outside DB txn without outbox** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

codeBased.question: How do you model **retry** for a failing record without blocking `poll()` forever?
codeBased.answer: Use **non-blocking** retry topics, **pause**/**resume** partitions, or **delegate** to **async** processing with **bounded** queues. **Blocking** the poll loop trips **max.poll.interval** and **kills** the consumer.

codeBased.question: What is a typical **Connect** **Single Message Transform** risk?
codeBased.answer: **SMTs** can **drop** fields silently, **rename** keys, or **break** schema evolution if not tested. Keep transforms **visible** in **config** reviews and **contract** tests.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Payments** duplicated during **broker** flap.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze payouts; reconcile ledger.

**(2) Root cause** Consumer **not** idempotent on **retry**.

**(3) Fix** **Unique** constraint + **manual** **adjustment** playbook.

**(4) Prevention** Game day **retry** hammer tests. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** **Payments** duplicated during **broker** flap.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Design:** **EOS** marketing slide but **MySQL** writes not **idempotent**.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Reality check workshop.

**(2) Root cause** Slide ≠ **architecture**.

**(3) Fix** Adopt **outbox** + **unique** keys.

**(4) Prevention** ADR signed by **finance**. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Transaction logs.

**(2) Root cause** Ordering wrong.

**(3) Fix** Commit **after** persist or **txn** **outbox**.

**(4) Prevention** Integration tests. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Legal asks source.

**(2) Root cause** Offset-only dedupe used.

**(3) Fix** Switch to **business** **keys** in **exports**.

**(4) Prevention** Retention policy doc. seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Writes collapse.

**(2) Root cause** Monotonic bigserial primary key hotspot.

**(3) Fix** Shard **dedupe** or use **UUID** keys with careful indexing.

**(4) Prevention** Capacity modeling. seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Duplicates expected.

**(2) Root cause** Two topics same events.

**(3) Fix** Consumer handles **eventId** globally.

**(4) Prevention** Cutover checklist. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

wrongAnswers: **EOS** in Kafka means **no** duplicates anywhere — **Correction:** **Side** **effects** still need **design**.
**UUID** always solves dedupe — **Correction:** Need **unique** constraint + handling.
**Offsets** are stable business keys — **Correction:** **Replay** changes offsets.
**Synchronous** HTTP to partner inside consumer is EOS — **Correction:** **Timeouts** create uncertainty.

sections.title: MCQ Quiz — Delivery Semantics & EOS
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — Delivery Semantics & EOS. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] Which statement best describes **at-most-once** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **at-most-once** belongs to the operational model for **Delivery Semantics & EOS**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **at-least-once**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **at-least-once** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **exactly-once**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **exactly-once** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] Which statement best describes **idempotent sink** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **idempotent sink** belongs to the operational model for **Delivery Semantics & EOS**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] You see misconfiguration around **outbox pattern** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **outbox pattern** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **dead letter queue** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **at-most-once**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **at-most-once** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] Which statement best describes **at-least-once** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **at-least-once** belongs to the operational model for **Delivery Semantics & EOS**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **exactly-once**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **exactly-once** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **idempotent sink**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **idempotent sink** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **outbox pattern** belongs to the operational model for **Delivery Semantics & EOS**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Delivery Semantics & EOS] You see misconfiguration around **dedupe key** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **dedupe key** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

sections.title: Delivery Semantics & EOS — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Term | Recall | Recall |
| At-least-once | Kafka default + retries | Kafka default + retries |
| Idempotent consumer | Safe replays | Safe replays |
| Outbox | Atomic DB + event | Atomic DB + event |
| Business id | Stable dedupe key | Stable dedupe key |
| Tx consume-produce | Kafka EOS scope | Kafka EOS scope |
| DLQ | Poison path | Poison path |
| Offset commit order | Loss vs dup trade | Loss vs dup trade |
| Saga | Cross-service workflow | Cross-service workflow |
| CDC replay | Needs dedupe | Needs dedupe |
| Reconciliation | Finance hourly jobs | Finance hourly jobs |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
